records.mapped_scope: Role and permissible use of tissue cross-reactivity evidence in species selection
records.analyst_rationale: Part I 3.3 treats tissue cross-reactivity evidence as part of species relevance for monoclonal antibodies (kr.part1.3_3.004), and allows an epitope-negative species to remain of some relevance where comparable unintentional tissue cross-reactivity to humans is demonstrated (kr.part1.3_3.005). Addendum body records kr.part2.2_1.007 and kr.part2.2_1.008 carry the primary species-selection guidance: animal-tissue tissue cross-reactivity is of limited value for species selection, and may be used only in specific cases where other approaches cannot identify a pharmacologically relevant species. That body source unit explicitly references Note 1 (see the contextual cross-reference below), and Note 1 records kr.part2.notes.005 and kr.part2.notes.014 materially define the restriction: full-panel animal-tissue tissue cross-reactivity is not recommended, and selected animal-tissue evaluation provides only conditional supplemental information (when there is unexpected binding in human tissues). Including the Note 1 records as Addendum endpoints does not mean Note 1 independently amends the Parent; the body records and the referenced Note 1 records together form the complete Addendum meaning for this mapping. Taken together, the Addendum neither contradicts nor replaces the Parent guidance: it narrows the scope and evidentiary role of animal-tissue tissue cross-reactivity in species selection, so the relation type is narrows.
records.contextual_cross_reference_note: kr.part2.2_1.008 derives from source unit su.part2.2_1.005, which carries the explicit reference xref.part2.2_1.001 with raw text '(see Note 1)'. The Note 1 target is not model-resolvable (target_id=null, resolution_status=needs_review), but the raw reference and the related Note 1 source units are preserved, so the contextual linkage is present and was reviewed before assigning the relation type.
